heading: Annual registration, etc.
label: 6057 Annual registration, etc.

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: Within such period after the end of a plan year as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe, the plan administrator (within the meaning of section 414(g)) of each plan to which the vesting standards of section 203 of part 2 of subtitle B of title I of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 applies for such plan year shall file a registration statement with the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: the name of the plan,

children.label: B
children.text: the name and address of the plan administrator,

children.label: i
children.text: who, during such plan year, separated from the service covered by the plan,

children.label: ii
children.text: who is entitled to a deferred vested benefit under the plan as of the end of such plan year, and

children.label: iii
children.text: with respect to whom retirement benefits were not paid under the plan during such plan year,

children.label: D
children.text: the nature, amount, and form of the deferred vested benefit to which such participant is entitled, and

children.label: E
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Notification of change in status
children.label: b Notification of change in status

children.text: any change in the name of the plan,
any change in the name or address of the plan administrator,
the termination of the plan, or
the merger or consolidation of the plan with any other plan or its division into two or more plans.
any plan to which subsection (a) applies, and
any other plan (including any governmental plan or church plan (within the meaning of section 414)),

children.heading: Transmission of information to Commissioner of Social Security
children.label: d Transmission of information to Commissioner of Social Security
children.text: The Secretary shall transmit copies of any statements, notifications, reports, or other information obtained by him under this section to the Commissioner of Social Security.

children.heading: Individual statement to participant
children.label: e Individual statement to participant
children.text: Each plan administrator required to file a registration statement under subsection (a) shall, before the expiration of the time prescribed for the filing of such registration statement, also furnish to each participant described in subsection (a)(2)(C) an individual statement setting forth the information with respect to such participant required to be contained in such registration statement. Such statement shall also include a notice to the participant of any benefits which are forfeitable if the participant dies before a certain date.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, after consultation with the Commissioner of Social Security, may prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.

children.heading: Plans to which more than one employer contributes
children.label: 2 Plans to which more than one employer contributes
children.text: This section shall apply to any plan to which more than one employer is required to contribute only to the extent provided in regulations prescribed under this subsection.

children.heading: 403(b) multiple employer plans treated as one plan
children.label: g 403(b) multiple employer plans treated as one plan
children.text: In the case of annuity contracts to which this section applies and to which section 403(b) applies by reason of the plan under which such contracts are purchased meeting the requirements of paragraph (15) thereof, such plan shall be treated as a single plan for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Cross references
children.label: h Cross references
children.text: For provisions relating to penalties for failure to register or furnish statements required by this section, see section 6652(d) and section 6690.For coordination between Department of the Treasury and the Department of Labor with regard to administration of this section, see section 3004 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974.
